  "description": "Neutratech, a subsidiary of Coway, is a South Korean company primarily into designing and producing water and air purification devices. We have been in existence for over 32years and have won an award as the 6th best company in direct sales in the world, pulling in a net worth of $3.8billion.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales &amp; Marketing Executive

Job Location: Gudu, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	Are you passionate about sales, marketing, and building meaningful customer relationships? Do you want to work with a fast-growing multinational company that rewards hard work and performance? Then this opportunity is for you!


Requirements


	Candidates with HND or Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree qualifications
	Strong communication and negotiation skills
	Smart, confident, and target-driven individuals
	Must reside in Abuja
	Being mobile is an added advantage


Why Join Us?


	&nbsp;Salary: N250,000 &ndash; N400,000 per month
	Attractive commissions and performance incentives
	Career advancement opportunities
	Professional and supportive work environment
	Continuous growth and training opportunities


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: recruitment@neutratech.com using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Sales & Marketing Executive Job at Neutratech




        
    

        
        Neutratech, a subsidiary of Coway, is a South Korean company primarily into designing and producing water and air purification devices. We have been in existence for over 32years and have won an award as the 6th best company in direct sales in the world, pulling in a net worth of $3.8billion.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales & Marketing Executive

Job Location: Gudu, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

            
    




	Are you passionate about sales, marketing, and building meaningful customer relationships? Do you want to work with a fast-growing multinational company that rewards hard work and performance? Then this opportunity is for you!


Requirements


	Candidates with HND or Bachelor’s Degree qualifications
	Strong communication and negotiation skills
	Smart, confident, and target-driven individuals
	Must reside in Abuja
	Being mobile is an added advantage


Why Join Us?


	 Salary: N250,000 – N400,000 per month
	Attractive commissions and performance incentives
	Career advancement opportunities
	Professional and supportive work environment
	Continuous growth and training opportunities


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Executive Assistant",
  "description": "Accretio Africa, an HR Consulting Firm, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Assistant
Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Work Mode - Hybrid (3 days onsite)

Job Summary


	Are you highly organized, tech-savvy, and great at keeping things on track? We&#39;re looking for an Executive Assistant to support a busy Interior Designer with project coordination, scheduling, research, and day-to-day operations.


What You&#39;ll Do


	Manage calendars, meetings, and communications.
	Coordinate with clients, vendors, and contractors.
	Keep project files, documents, and records organized.
	Conduct research and source materials for projects.
	Support project execution and attend meetings or site visits when required.
	Learn and adapt quickly to new tools and systems.


What We&#39;re Looking For


	Strong experience with Notion and task management tools.
	Highly tech-savvy and able to pick up new software quickly.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (especially PowerPoint) and Canva.
	Excellent organizational, communication, and follow-up skills.
	Detail-oriented, proactive, and able to work independently.
	Professional, calm, and confident in dealing with clients and stakeholders.
	If you&#39;re someone who enjoys bringing structure to fast-paced environments and loves getting things done, we&#39;d love to hear from you.

Executive Assistant Job at Accretio Africa




        
    

        
        Accretio Africa, an HR Consulting Firm, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Assistant
Job Location: Victoria Island, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time
Work Mode - Hybrid (3 days onsite)

Job Summary


	Are you highly organized, tech-savvy, and great at keeping things on track? We're looking for an Executive Assistant to support a busy Interior Designer with project coordination, scheduling, research, and day-to-day operations.


What You'll Do
     

            
    




	Manage calendars, meetings, and communications.
	Coordinate with clients, vendors, and contractors.
	Keep project files, documents, and records organized.
	Conduct research and source materials for projects.
	Support project execution and attend meetings or site visits when required.
	Learn and adapt quickly to new tools and systems.


What We're Looking For


	Strong experience with Notion and task management tools.
	Highly tech-savvy and able to pick up new software quickly.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office (especially PowerPoint) and Canva.
	Excellent organizational, communication, and follow-up skills.
	Detail-oriented, proactive, and able to work independently.
	Professional, calm, and confident in dealing with clients and stakeholders.
	If you're someone who enjoys bringing structure to fast-paced environments and loves getting things done, we'd love to hear from you.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Assistant to the Office of the Medical Director and Clinical Services",
  "description": "ECWA Hospital, Egbe, a division of ECWA (Evangelical Church Winning All), is a rural teaching hospital established in 1952 and located in Egbe, Kogi State, Nigeria. The hospital serves a 100-mile radius of villages and small towns in a poor area. It is a 110-bed hospital on a 32-acre complex that includes 5 wards for patients, 3 Operating rooms, a Maternity ward, an Under-fives clinic, an Eye care center, an HIV/AIDs counseling center, a Physiotherapy clinic, and a dental clinic. The hospital runs a 24-hour emergency and intensive care unit. The hospital has an X-ray department with digital radiology including portable X-ray, outpatient and bedside ultrasound services, electrocardiography, gastroscopy, and HSG.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant to the Office of the Medical Director and Clinical Services

Job Location: Kogi
Job type: Full-time

The Position


	Reporting to the Medical Director, the Assistant will provide support to the Offlce of the Medical Director and Clinical Services of the Hospital. The candidate will perform a variety of administrative and clerical tasks, implementing office procedures to support the efficient operation of the Office of the Medical Director and Clinical Services of the hospital.


Qualifications


	Minimum of ND qualification in Secretarial duties, Data Management, Statistics, Social Sciences, or any related discipline.
	Proven experience as an office assistant, or in another relevant administrative role.
	Proficient computer uses and knowledge of Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, working knowledge of office equipment.
	Thorough understanding of office management procedures.
	Excellent organizational and time management skills.
	Analytical abilities and aptitude in problem-solving to incorporate new and effective ways to achieve better results.
	Ability to work diligently under pressure and maintain confidentiality.
	Ability to balance multiple priorities and take instructions from various parties.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills with attention to detail.
	Customer service orientation.
	Graphics design abilities Will be an added advantage.


Experience:


	Minimum of two years of relevant experience.
	An attitude of serving and a desire to teach and train others.

Assistant to the Office of the Medical Director and Clinical Services Job at ECWA Hospital




        
    

        
        ECWA Hospital, Egbe, a division of ECWA (Evangelical Church Winning All), is a rural teaching hospital established in 1952 and located in Egbe, Kogi State, Nigeria. The hospital serves a 100-mile radius of villages and small towns in a poor area. It is a 110-bed hospital on a 32-acre complex that includes 5 wards for patients, 3 Operating rooms, a Maternity ward, an Under-fives clinic, an Eye care center, an HIV/AIDs counseling center, a Physiotherapy clinic, and a dental clinic. The hospital runs a 24-hour emergency and intensive care unit. The hospital has an X-ray department with digital radiology including portable X-ray, outpatient and bedside ultrasound services, electrocardiography, gastroscopy, and HSG.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:
     

            
    



Job Position: Assistant to the Office of the Medical Director and Clinical Services

Job Location: Kogi
Job type: Full-time

The Position


	Reporting to the Medical Director, the Assistant will provide support to the Offlce of the Medical Director and Clinical Services of the Hospital. The candidate will perform a variety of administrative and clerical tasks, implementing office procedures to support the efficient operation of the Office of the Medical Director and Clinical Services of the hospital.


Qualifications


	Minimum of ND qualification in Secretarial duties, Data Management, Statistics, Social Sciences, or any related discipline.
	Proven experience as an office assistant, or in another relevant administrative role.
	Proficient computer uses and knowledge of Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, working knowledge of office equipment.
	Thorough understanding of office management procedures.
	Excellent organizational and time management skills.
	Analytical abilities and aptitude in problem-solving to incorporate new and effective ways to achieve better results.
	Ability to work diligently under pressure and maintain confidentiality.
	Ability to balance multiple priorities and take instructions from various parties.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills with attention to detail.
	Customer service orientation.
	Graphics design abilities Will be an added advantage.


Experience:


	Minimum of two years of relevant experience.
	An attitude of serving and a desire to teach and train others.
	Proficient computer skills and proven working knowledge of email, Word, Excel, and PowerPoint; working knowledge of office equipment.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

Human Resource Manager Job at ECWA Hospital




        
    

        
        ECWA Hospital, Egbe, a division of ECWA (Evangelical Church Winning All), is a rural teaching hospital established in 1952 and located in Egbe, Kogi State, Nigeria. The hospital serves a 100-mile radius of villages and small towns in a poor area. It is a 110-bed hospital on a 32-acre complex that includes 5 wards for patients, 3 Operating rooms, a Maternity ward, an Under-fives clinic, an Eye care center, an HIV/AIDs counseling center, a Physiotherapy clinic, and a dental clinic. The hospital runs a 24-hour emergency and intensive care unit. The hospital has an X-ray department with digital radiology including portable X-ray, outpatient and bedside ultrasound services, electrocardiography, gastroscopy, and HSG.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:
     

            
    



Job Position: Human Resource Manager

Job Location: Kogi
Job type: Full-time

The Role


	Reporting to the Administration Manager, the Human Resource Manager will oversee the recruiting and orientation of new staff;
	Support staff development and training; foster a team culture;
	Consult with the Management team on strategic planning relating to staff development;
	Serve as a link between the organization's management and its employees;
	Maintain knowledge of the Hospital's Conditions of service.


Qualifications & Experience:


	Bachelor of Science Degree in Human resources management
	National Youth Service or Exemption Certificate.
	Additional courses in subjects such as conflict management, industrial psychology , or Other related fields will be an asset.
	High Emotional Intelligence and Employee Coaching Skills.
	Full understanding Of the Nigeria Labour Law.
	Minimum 3 years of Human Resource Management or related work experience in organizing, directing, and leading others
	Demonstrated decision-making skills in an organizational environment
	Proven communication and interpersonal skills required to interact with managernent and employees; collaborate on teams and develop positive working relationships
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills with attention to details
	Abibty to work diligently under pressure and maintain cmfidentiality
	Proficient computer skills and knowledge of email, Word, Excel, PowerPoint; working knowledge of oflce

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accounts Officer

Job Location: Ozubulu, Anambra
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a diligent and detail-oriented Accounts Officer to join our team.
	The ideal candidate must possess a solid understanding of accounting principles, be capable of maintaining accurate financial records, reconciling stock and customer ledger balances, and supporting financial reporting processes.
	The role requires strong analytical ability, and the capacity to work under pressure while meeting deadlines.


Key Responsibilities


	Demonstrate a good understanding of Accounting Principles.
	Reconciliation of stock and customers&#39; ledger balances.
	Meet all financial reporting deadlines.


Requirements


	B.Sc./HND in Accounting or any related discipline.
	Proficiency in MS Excel and other accounting software.
	Strong attention to detail and accuracy.
	Good analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines.
	High level of integrity and professionalism.
	Prior experience in a manufacturing company is highly preferred.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hr@seahorselubricant.ng using &ldquo;Accounts Officer&rdquo; as the subject of the mail.

Accounts Officer Job at Seahorse Lubricant Industries Limited




        
    

        
        Seahorse Lubricant Industries Limited is a manufacturer of lubricating oil for internal combustion engines and mechanical systems.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Accounts Officer

Job Location: Ozubulu, Anambra
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking a diligent and detail-oriented Accounts Officer to join our team.
	The ideal candidate must possess a solid understanding of accounting principles, be capable of maintaining accurate financial records, reconciling stock and customer ledger balances, and supporting financial reporting processes.
	The role requires strong analytical ability, and the capacity to work under pressure while meeting deadlines.


Key Responsibilities


	Demonstrate a good understanding of Accounting Principles.
	Reconciliation of stock and customers' ledger balances.
	Meet all financial reporting deadlines.


Requirements


	B.Sc./HND in Accounting or any related discipline.
	Proficiency in MS Excel and other accounting software.
	Strong attention to detail and accuracy.
	Good analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines.
	High level of integrity and professionalism.
	Prior experience in a manufacturing company is highly preferred.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using “Accounts Officer” as the subject of the mail.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
